overview: Izzet Prowess leverages cheap creatures and noncreature spells to generate rapid +1/+1 and haste triggers, racing opponents before they stabilize. By cycling through low-cost artifacts and instants, the deck maintains board presence while applying relentless pressure. Success depends on efficient mana usage and sequencing spells to maximize combat impact.
win_conditions: Race opponents with haste-enabled prowess creatures
Overwhelm blockers through repeated spell-casting buffs
Generate value via token creation and mana dork synergy

keep_criteria: Hands with at least one cheap creature and a noncreature spell
Two mana sources and a low-CMC threat
Mishra's Bauble paired with an instant or sorcery
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (duals/fixers), plus at least one Prowess threat (Slickshot Show-Off, Cori-Steel Cutter) or a cheap cantrip/removal.
Prioritize hands that can cast 2+ spells on turn 1 or 2.
mulligan_criteria: Zero noncreature spells to trigger prowess
Three or more lands in a six-card hand
High-CMC cards without early mana acceleration
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only expensive spells, or hands lacking early interaction against aggro.

early_game: Play Mishra's Bauble and Cori-Steel Cutter on curve
Cast cheap instants or artifacts to trigger prowess before combat
Tap creatures for mana only when necessary to maintain board presence
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Mishra's Bauble (sacrifice for {2} damage or draw), or cast a cheap cantrip/removal.
If opening with a Prowess threat, play land, cast threat, attack to trigger Prowess + spell trigger.
Turn 2: Develop board with another cheap spell or threat, continue casting instants/sorceries to maximize Prowess damage.
Sequence lands to ensure double-blue or red sources for Bauble/fixing.
mid_game: Use Slickshot Show-Off's tap ability to push lethal or clear blockers
Cycle through cheap spells to keep prowess triggers active
Hold up mana for interaction if opponent attempts to stabilize
Prioritize casting cheap instants/sorceries over holding mana for Prowess triggers.
Use Bauble for card advantage when ahead, damage when behind.
Don't overcommit to board if opponent has sweepers
keep hand full of spells to trigger Prowess off threats.
Commit threats when you can protect them or when Prowess can close the game.
Hold back if opponent has sweepers or heavy removal.
late_game: Sacrifice Baubles for flying prowess tokens to bypass blockers
Maintain pressure with continuous spell casting to outvalue control decks
Pivot to defensive prowess triggers if opponent boards large threats
Prowess triggers from Slickshot/Cori-Steel, direct damage spells, and combat damage.
Win by chipping to lethal or attacking with multiple Prowess threats while casting spells in response.
Go all-in when you have 3+ spells in hand, a Prowess threat on board, and opponent has no sweepers or has tapped out.
Also commit when you can race combo decks to lethal before their turn.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Tap for colored mana to enable spells, but leave untapped when possible to attack with haste. Use its prowess to quickly grow into a combat threat.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Tap to buff allies or kill blockers. Sequence its ability after casting a noncreature spell to maximize combat damage.

key_cards.name: Mishra's Bauble
key_cards.notes: Tap for mana early to cast spells. Sacrifice for two mana to create a flying prowess token when you need to race or bypass blockers.

sequencing_tips: Cast noncreature spells before declaring attackers to trigger prowess for combat
Hold up mana for potential counterspells or tax effects before committing to attacks
Use tap abilities after prowess triggers resolve to avoid missing combat
Common mistake: Holding too many lands and not casting enough spells to trigger Prowess.
Always prioritize casting instants/sorceries over holding mana.
Remember that Prowess triggers even if the spell is countered (as long as it was cast).
Use Bauble for damage when behind, card draw when ahead.
Watch out for sweepers and plan spell sequencing around them.
Cori-Steel Cutter and Slickshot Show-Off are your primary win conditions

matchups.advice: Race aggressively before they resolve answers. Use flying tokens and haste to bypass counterspells.
Stabilize with cheap removal and use prowess triggers to outpace their curve. Prioritize blocking over attacking early.
Disrupt their game plan with cheap interaction. Use prowess to race their value engines before they establish board control.
Disrupt their key pieces with interaction. Use haste to apply pressure before they assemble their win condition.
Race them before they deploy large threats. Use cheap spells to trigger prowess and push damage early.

Use Prowess to cast spells in response to counterspells (e.g., cast Shock, let them counter, cast another spell to trigger Prowess again). Use Bauble for card advantage. Don't overcommit expensive spells; rely on cheap volume.
Out-value them with better sequencing. Prioritize casting spells to trigger Prowess before attacking. Use Bauble for card advantage. Win by having more spells in hand and better threat resilience.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Cyclonic Rift), counterspells, and removal.
Note when they tap out for big spells.
Track graveyard if they rely on flashback or dredge.
Watch for combo pieces (Amulet, Storm count, Blink targets).
